note: Shared oracle for streaming usage extraction. All 3 SDKs must produce identical usage from identical SSE bytes. These vectors exist because Python and Node silently reported NO usage for the single-usage-event case (the OpenAI-compatible shape) while Go handled it correctly: both gated the SSE path on the text containing a newline, so a lone 'data: {...}' line fell through to a JSON parser with the 'data: ' prefix still attached. A per-language test suite passed throughout. Captured shapes are real provider bytes where noted.

cases.name: openai_compatible_single_usage_final_chunk
cases.note: REAL bytes captured from NVIDIA NIM (meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct) 2026-07-17. Only the final chunk carries usage and intermediates omit the field entirely — one usage-bearing line, no newline after the join. This is the case that was broken in Python and Node.

cases.name: openai_null_usage_intermediates
cases.note: OpenAI proper sends "usage":null in every intermediate chunk and the real numbers in the final one. MAX-merge must ignore the nulls rather than reset on them.

cases.name: anthropic_split_input_output
cases.note: Anthropic reports input tokens at message_start and output tokens at message_delta, repeating fields. Summing would double-count input; MAX per field is correct.

cases.name: no_usage_anywhere
cases.note: A stream without include_usage emits no usage at all. Extraction must report NOT FOUND so the caller commits its reserved estimate rather than zero — the denial-of-wallet path.

cases.name: single_line_no_trailing_newline
cases.note: The minimal reproduction: exactly one data line, no trailing newline. Newline-gated SSE detection fails here and returns nothing.
